chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No. D-1400 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on office objections at Flag „A‟. 2. For hearing of main case. 07.11.2023. Mr. Niaz Hussain Maitlo, Advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Irshad Hussain Dharejo, Advocate for respondents Nos.6 &7. Mr. Manzoor Hussain Larik, Advocate for Respondents Nos. 8 & 9. Mr. Ghulam Mustafa Abro, AAG. -.-.-.- The Petitioner, professes to be an officer of Shah Abdul Lateef University, Khairpur, working as its Assistant Register (Teach), and has invoked the jurisdiction of this Court under Article 199 of Constitution impugning a strike called by private persons arrayed as the Respondents Nos. 6 to 9, who are said to have made such call under the banner of the SALU Employee Welfare Association. In that backdrop, it had been prayed that this Court be pleased inter alia: “(a) To declare the act of respondents Nos. 6 to 9 are illegal, direct the respondents to stop the protest/strike and open the Administration Block immediately. (b) To declare Employees Association and Officer Association, as deregistered/cancelled organizations as per letter issued by respondent No.4 No.ADD: SW/KHP/264/2023 Dated 31.08.2023 and respondent No.3 issued notification No.03SO(COORD) /SW/03(55/2019/1075) Karachi dated 25.10.2019. (c) To direct the respondents Nos. 4 and 5 to take the legal action against the respondents Nos. 06 to 09 and maintain the Law and order situation in the University.” 2 Having considered the matter, we have observed that the Petition has not been brought on behalf of the University, which is not even a party to the proceedings, and are of the view that the petitioner is not competent to advance the grievances sought to be raised or seek the relief elicited.

Ghulam Mustafa Abro, AAG. -.-.-.- The Petitioner, professes to be an officer of Shah Abdul Lateef University, Khairpur, working as its Assistant Register (Teach), and has invoked the jurisdiction of this Court under Article 199 of Constitution impugning a strike called by private persons arrayed as the Respondents Nos. 6 to 9, who are said to have made such call under the banner of the SALU Employee Welfare Association. In that backdrop, it had been prayed that this Court be pleased inter alia: “(a) To declare the act of respondents Nos. 6 to 9 are illegal, direct the respondents to stop the protest/strike and open the Administration Block immediately. (b) To declare Employees Association and Officer Association, as deregistered/cancelled organizations as per letter issued by respondent No.4 No.ADD: SW/KHP/264/2023 Dated 31.08.2023 and respondent No.3 issued notification No.03SO(COORD) /SW/03(55/2019/1075) Karachi dated 25.10.2019. (c) To direct the respondents Nos. 4 and 5 to take the legal action against the respondents Nos. 06 to 09 and maintain the Law and order situation in the University.” 2 Having considered the matter, we have observed that the Petition has not been brought on behalf of the University, which is not even a party to the proceedings, and are of the view that the petitioner is not competent to advance the grievances sought to be raised or seek the relief elicited. Be that as it may, it transpires that as per the reports submitted in the matter, certain round of negotiations are said to have ensued, culminating in the end of the strike in question. Furthermore, certain police deployments have been made at the University in order to safeguard against any untoward future incident. In view of the foregoing, the petition stands dismissed accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE Irfan/PA
